item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Page has been updated to be consistent; please notify me (via wikidot PM) or another mod/admin if further changes are necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This line in the "Writing SCPs/Other" section in reference to qualifications for creating an author page —

Once you have three positively-rated SCPs and/or tales. Any combination of SCPs and tales are fine, so long as the total number is three.

contradicts with this one on the Authors' Pages:

If you've created at least three successful pages (including SCP articles, tales, GoI-format documents, site dossiers, and artwork pages), please take the time to create an author page and take credit for your work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Page has been updated; thank you for bringing that to staff attention!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Under the tab, SCPs and Stories and the section titled Which SCPs should I read?, there is a line that says “…the latest series, Series 5.”

Should the word Series 5 be changed to the title, Series 6. I don’t know if Series 6 is to gain more entries in order to be added to this or if it yet to be changed. I understand that Series 6 just came out, but I don’t want people to forget to change that line. Intentional or not, I just wanted to point it out, just in case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My headcanon for the in-universe Foundation response is, "okay, tell us about the SCP you saw", followed by "thanks for the information, tomorrow you'll just go back to your normal life like you never saw us."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well… we are the Foundation my finred… so it is something we'd say….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
- I saw an SCP!
- No you didn't!
Sounds like something foundation would say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does the wiki get funding?

item.title: Re: Is this correct?
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, needs an update. I'll bring this up with the other admins.

ETA: with advice from DrMann and wording from Decibelles, that question has been updated!

item.title: Is this correct?
item.content:encoded:
Is SCP-173 based on the Weeping Angels? Or the Endermen?

Nope. SCP-173 was created in 2007, a couple of months before the Weeping Angels

I thought recent evidence from the 4chan archives has disproven this, with the original post of SCP-173 being a couple of weeks after "Blink" aired? Has this changed again?

https://lostmediawiki.com/SCP-173_(found_4chan_post;_2007)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God,I love this site

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, this FAQ seems out of date.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn’t SCP-173 made about a week after the Weeping Angels? I saw something about this on a post on reddit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
shouldn't the password be there, or atleast the name of the person you can pm for the password?

I think this information should be put on the Visual Records wiki, but not necessarily this FAQ. I'll give Roget a nudge about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My assumption is that the site was once open to everyone, but is now closed off. Even if that's not the case, shouldn't the password be there, or atleast the name of the person you can pm for the password?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I take it that this tidbit needs to be updated?

How so? Even if there's a password involved, doesn't the statement "anyone can join" still apply?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can also post on Visual Records — anyone can join, even if they're not a member here.

Considering that the site now requires a password to join, I take it that this tidbit needs to be updated?

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Zachman1029, this sort of discussion belongs on the corresponding discussion thread(s) of the article(s) involved, not the general site FAQ.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would not Enderman be more similar to 096 than 173?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone who has permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As far as I remember, all pages tagged with "guide" are written by staff members.

Edit: Or former staff members, or users who were not staff at the time of posting, but are staff now (though I think it's just one case like that; someone can check me on my facts).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who can and cannot write a guide?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Why is everyone mean to me?"
Dude, I read this FAQ page like 20 times and I can't remember the answer.

Sorry to all moderators (and admins) I pissed off due I was a sissy.

item.title: Re: MASSIVE FAQ REWRITE
item.content:encoded:
I think the issue is that wardens doesn't sound quite right. Caretakers does, but it has three syllables so it sounds "off" in the cadence of the sentence.

I like what you went with. That's just my $.02 on why waffling between those two happens when you try to compare them. lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They'd already mostly stopped asking, for whatever reason — and it wasn't because they were actually reading the former FAQ.

If they actually do start asking again, maybe we'll add it back in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the main reason it was removed is because most of those questions actually get asked anymore (and some never did), and the people who should read those things don't (or don't think they apply to them).

Also, we're trying to move further away from the whole 'staff act like jackasses at people for sport' thing. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You took out the 110-Montauk answer. Now people are going to be asking about it alllll over again. :I

item.title: Re: MASSIVE FAQ REWRITE
item.content:encoded:
Going with 'wardens'.

EDIT: Actually, I keep going back and forth on which one flows better. But I'll pick one for now; they both work :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I'm just sad because I found it amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep. Staff's thought it over. I personally felt like it wasn't necessary because it sort of nudged towards harsh being an indication of other qualities (e.g. knowing your stuff well, being an experienced writer) that really didn't relate.

If anyone's worried about harsh answers, they can PM a staff member for a quick "does this have a shot on the forums/chat?" before going for open critique. If anyone feels that critique is harsh to the point of being personal attacks, staff will step in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gah! They got rid of Harsh Answers!

item.title: Re: MASSIVE FAQ REWRITE
item.content:encoded:
Wanna say "keepers", but it's kinda similar to "holders"… But I like the way "keepers of dreams" sounds on its own.

Oo… maybe…

"We are the guardians of wonders, and the keepers of dreams."

…?

item.title: Re: MASSIVE FAQ REWRITE
item.content:encoded: Caretakers of dreams? Wardens?

item.title: Re: MASSIVE FAQ REWRITE
item.content:encoded:
This is such a great response, though.

How would you suggest that be rephrased? It's a holdover from years ago. Just has to sound cool. Poetic like. "Guardians?"

item.title: Re: MASSIVE FAQ REWRITE
item.content:encoded:
We are the holders of wonders, and the crafters of dreams.

The Foundation does not craft dreams, sir.

  • They measure dreams.
  • They analyze dreams.
  • They secure dreams.
  • They contain dreams,
  • They protect the world from dreams.
  • They write reports about dreams.
  • They lock dreams away, never to be heard from again.

I don't know where that "crafting" nonsense came from…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hitting on other users is a terrible idea.

You'll never get in the way of my love for <insert randomly selected user here>, NEVER, NEVER, NEVER…

...
Now, seriously

I'm actually quite happy the way that answer is worded. We can get quite silly in chat at times, but people should understand those are stupid exchanges among acquaintances WHO ARE IN ON THE JOKE (and who would get the exact same axe if they tried that on a stranger or if they were making even their friends uncomfortable).

As whole, I feel this covers pretty much everything an official FAQ should cover. I will miss the pooryoric/Brightian charm of "Harsh Answers", though (I understand, though, why it was cut).

item.title: MASSIVE FAQ REWRITE
item.content:encoded:
Rewritten from the ground up. Go read it.

Thank you in particular to TroyL, kaktus, weizhong, Kalinin, Blaroth, Crayne, Photosynthetic, and Zyn. And everyone else who looked over this in chat. (Let me know if I forgot to credit you.)

item.title: Re: Math Error
item.content:encoded: Didn't know that. But it's still an interesting thing that the number lined up like that so perfectly yesterday. =D

item.title: Re: Math Error
item.content:encoded: You can get that stat by clicking your name, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added fun - do a Google search for it.

"I didn't think that was possible."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
>:|

EDIT: Apparently just writing ">:|" produces a totally blank post. Who knew.

item.title: Math Error
item.content:encoded:
That should be:
100(NumberOfPages - 1) + NumberOfUsersOnLastPage

=D

E: So, I decided to look up when I joined on that page… It appears that as of today, I joined 387 days ago… o.0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's… deceptive. The number reflects everyone that has signed up ever and has not:

  1. deleted their account
  2. quit from JUST the wiki
  3. been kicked by mods.

Lots of people create accounts then wander off. Some wander back in.

There's not really a way to find out the number of "active" members.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: meh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd never seen that page before, somehow. Wow - there are a lot more than I thought! Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a math thing, actually. On the Site Members page, you can see at a glance how many pages there are of usernames. A full page has 100 users, so the site has 100*(number of pages) plus however many are on the most recent page. Just add them up for a quick count.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me - so I'll ask here - is there a way to know exactly how many site members there are? Or is that an Aelanna thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Forgot to mention, but I gave this page a "quick" update. It still needs a full rewrite, but hopefully it's better for now.

Incomplete changelog:

Overall

  • Cleaned up a large number of answers for clarity.
    • Deleted Mary Sue references for being confusing, redundant, and generally ignored anyway for male characters.
  • Added link at the bottom top to #site17

Site Tab

  • Changed answer about expanded media, joining just to lurk, to reflect our community's more favorable position towards these things.
  • Told people to ask in chat for RP info (since this changes a lot).
  • Suggested that people read newer SCPs, not just old ones.
  • Shortened the password questions because they weren't as necessary. (May need to tweak those again.)

SCPs Tab

  • Removed the implied recommendation to coldpost.
  • Removed recommendation to not write a thing if 'we've already got one'.
  • Clarified that 'there is no canon' doesn't really mean 'no canon'.
  • Updated the D-Class explanation to reflect (and link to) the new Security Clearance Levels page.
  • Tweaked the amnestic/amnesiac explanation to clarify that the latter is outmoded (but still usable if you must).
  • Updated the crosslinks section to reflect (and link to) the section in How To Write An SCP.
  • Clarified the Montauk explanation to be both more blunt, and to clarify that we really don't want to hear gaslighting about it or speculation/theories on what it really is (which is and honestly has always been the biggest issue).

Critique Tab

  • Cut down the 'why so mean' answer, linked to the Criticism Policy (a severely outdated version was previously linked).
  • Removed the 'meh' answer since we don't do 'meh' anymore.

Wiki Etiquette Tab

  • Again, changed the password questions. (If you use compare versions here it looks like I changed a lot more, but it's mainly re-arranging or tweaking for clarity.)

Chat Etiquette Tab

  • Cut out stuff that's redundant with the chat guide. Added a link to the chat guide.
  • Removed the forbidden topics question, because with the exception of rape (already in the chat guide), we don't forbid topics anymore.

Staff Etiquette Tab

  • Rehauled the senior staff explanation for accuracy / clarity, since the answer was almost completely wrong except for the junior staff explanation, which also tried to explain the rest of staff structure all by itself (!).

Harsh Answers Tab

  • Cut down the opening framing/questions due to not being necessary.
  • Cut down some of the unnecessary wanky shit from the answers overall.
  • Removed us saying that we doubt you'll move up through the ranks, given that we've put a lot of (semi-successful) work into changing that attitude amongst staff, and don't need our FAQ reflecting circa 2012 attitudes about this.
  • Removed the FURRIES TEH GROSS / paranoia about revealing sexuality answer. Since almost none of us give a shit if you're a furry (or anything else) anymore. Pretty much none of this was a reflection of current community attitude.
  • Added in an answer reflecting our attitude towards 'pointlessly talking about rape' because as a community we've gotten as sick of that as we once were about furries, yet nothing in our official non-chat documentation reflected that. (Ironically I was able to re-use some of the original OH NO FURRIES language. We have weird-ass historical standards.)
  • Added in an answer about not creeping on/derping on female members, to reflect the fact that our community now has a much larger percentage of non-male members and this is getting more and more relevant.
  • Removed the goofy 'where do babies come from' question, since it was the kind of thing that was funny in chat, but not really stuck in the official FAQ for years.

Everyone else, and other staff in particular: Please let me know any new suggestions for changes, or if you disagree with changes made. I've tried to reflect present community standards in this update, and this isn't any sort of finalized rewrite.

Also, when this is rewritten, it will likely be done from scratch, so keep that in mind too if it affects anything. (IE, there's a reason I did this in a stopgap manner.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This still seems to be the case.

Also noticed somewhere mentioning the site being "three, four years old" when it's six, going on seven, now. Don't know if we want to be having to update that every year, but going through this periodically is probably a good idea on its own anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't remember the harsh answers section being there when I did my required reading, it's not new is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How many people do you see on #site17? You should be able to ask a chat op or a staff member there for help on getting to #site19.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm joining through SynIRC, and I'll try #site17.

EDIT: I can get on #site17.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you join #site17? Also, are you joining through SynIRC? If you can get into #site17, someone will help you from there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've registered my Nick and it won't let me join #site19.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's all. I didn't realize that canceling out malicious downvotes was still a thing, my bad.

Look again. Think.

As for this, I was thinking of it from the perspective of malicious downvoting being something that it'd be bad to wrongly accuse someone of, though I do see where you're coming from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, staff's gonna take a look at it soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. This is out of date AFAIK self-up voting is never allowed.

This is incorrect. We haven't had anyone bring this matter up recently, but self-upvoting with the blessing of staff to cancel out an obviously malicious downvote is still a thing.

(Answer) Look again. Think.

I don't agree with this. People should feel comfortable contacting staff about questions they have about the site, not tripping over themselves to check if the query is without a doubt legitimate. If someone's not sure about a downvote, they should feel fine about just going up to a staff member, without staring at the screen to convince themselves. There shouldn't be any need for hesitation. Staff are here to answer questions.

I'll wait for the rest of the suggestions you have, and I'll bring it to the rest of staff when you're done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going read the entire FAQ and post suggestions here (i.e. This will be edited a bunch of times)

Is this a roleplaying site?

Add a link to the RP website.

Why am I being asked for my password?

Add that if a site member actually does this then tell staff.

Mainlist SCPs have to be scary, right?

Maybe add a couple of examples of SCPs that do this well. Maybe SCP-1915 or SCP-1230?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last page of the FAQ's is kinda outdated, now that there are well over 2000 SCP's, and nearly 9700 members. Maybe it should be updated?
